She never lost her bond with the land. She painted the things that were most important to her, which were simplified portrayals of nature. She was moved by the Western desert's bleak landscape and broad skies, which influenced her paintings. She used elements of the desert like cows' skulls, horizon lines, flowers, rocks, etc., as subjects. She continued to paint these themes until her death.
